description_long: This is a small, standalone public toilet structure made primarily of vertical blue-painted wooden planks. The front features a single door with a heart-shaped cutout for ventilation and privacy, which is slightly ajar, revealing a dark interior. The roof is a single sloped panel made of weathered wood, extending beyond the walls to provide some protection from rain. The back of the structure is decorated with a simple, hand-drawn figure, adding a touch of character to the otherwise utilitarian design. The sides are plain, with the wood grain and vertical paneling clearly visible. The overall appearance is rustic and functional, suitable for placement in parks, rural areas, or community spaces.
description: A small blue wooden public toilet with a sloped roof, heart-cutout door, and hand-drawn figure on the back. Rustic and functional, with weathered wood and simple construction.
description_view_0: Front view showing the blue wooden door with a heart-shaped cutout, partially open, revealing the dark interior.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the sloped wooden roof and plain blue plank wall, with the door visible at an angle.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the hand-drawn figure on the blue wooden planks and the full width of the sloped roof.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the blue plank wall and the open door from a different angle, emphasizing the roof's overhang.
attribute_signage: Heart-shaped cutout on the door; hand-drawn figure on the back wall; no formal signage.
attribute_roof_structure: Single sloped roof made of weathered wood, with an overhang on all sides.
